title: 10 possible side effects of Urtica dioica (Nettle) if used improperly
intro: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, has been associated with various health benefits when used properly, but it can also have adverse effects if used improperly. One possible side effect of misuse is contact dermatitis, a skin irritation that can occur when touching the plant's stinging hairs. Ingesting large amounts of Urtica dioica can lead to gastrointestinal disturbances, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, there have been reports of kidney damage associated with the long-term consumption of the plant. Pregnant women and individuals with chronic kidney disease should avoid using Urtica dioica altogether, as it may worsen their conditions. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using Urtica dioica to ensure it is safe and appropriate for your individual health needs.
